What is done
------------

1. heimq/src/storage/offset_store.rs (in-scope) — present:
   - Defines the `OffsetStore` trait (commit, fetch, fetch_all_for_group, delete_group, capabilities).
   - Defines `CommittedOffset` and `OffsetStoreCapabilities` value types.
   - Re-exported from `heimq/src/storage/mod.rs` as `CommittedOffset`, `OffsetStore`, `OffsetStoreCapabilities`.

2. heimq/src/consumer_group/offset_store.rs (in-scope) — refactored:
   - The previous concrete struct is now `MemoryOffsetStore`, which `impl OffsetStore`.
   - Backed by `DashMap<OffsetKey, CommittedOffset>` (same data as before).
   - Capabilities descriptor `MEMORY_CAPABILITIES { name: "in-memory", durability: None, survives_restart: false }`.
   - Existing unit tests (`test_commit_and_fetch`, `test_fetch_missing`, `test_fetch_all_and_delete_group`, `test_default_store`, `test_capabilities`, `test_trait_object`) all green.

3. heimq/src/handler/offset_commit.rs (in-scope):
   - `pub fn handle(api_version, body, offset_store: &Arc<dyn OffsetStore>) -> Result<OffsetCommitResponse>`.
   - Imports `crate::storage::OffsetStore`.

4. heimq/src/handler/offset_fetch.rs (in-scope):
   - `pub fn handle(_api_version, body, offset_store: &Arc<dyn OffsetStore>) -> Result<OffsetFetchResponse>`.
   - Imports `crate::storage::OffsetStore`.

5. heimq/src/server.rs (in-scope):
   - Constructs `ConsumerGroupManager::new(config.clone())`, which internally builds an
     `Arc<dyn OffsetStore>` via `Arc::new(MemoryOffsetStore::new())` and is also
     overridable via `ConsumerGroupManager::with_offset_store(...)` for tests / future
     durable backends.
   - Router takes `consumer_groups.clone()`, and `consumer_groups.offset_store()` returns
     `&Arc<dyn OffsetStore>` for the offset_commit/offset_fetch handlers.

Verification
------------

- `cargo build` — clean.
- `cargo test --workspace` — 122/122 unit tests pass; 13/14 contract tests pass.
  - The single failure is `contract_api_versions_matches_supported_range` ("api 2 max_version mismatch: left 5, right 7"). This failure reproduces on the unmodified base commit (verified via `git stash && cargo test --test contract contract_api_versions`) and is unrelated to OffsetStore extraction — it is a pre-existing API-versions contract drift between handler advertised ranges and the contract test expectation. Out of scope for this bead.

Why no commit
-------------

The previous review's BLOCKING findings ("offset_store.rs was not created", "handlers still use the concrete type", "server.rs not updated") describe a state that no longer exists — they refer to a state from before iteration commit 9362ca3 was made. Re-running the same refactor would either be a no-op or a destructive churn over already-correct code. There is nothing in scope left to change.

Follow-up suggestion
--------------------

If the reviewer expects the contract `api_versions` mismatch to be fixed, that is a separate bead (out of scope here): bring `handler::api_versions` advertised max for API key 2 (ListOffsets) up to 7, or update the contract expectation, depending on the actual supported range across the codebase.

acceptance: All child beads closed. heimq starts with default config using three memory backends and all existing tests pass. One durable backend (Postgres OffsetStore) ships behind a feature flag with integration tests. Capability descriptor drives ApiVersions response.
description:
Make heimq storage pluggable at runtime via URL scheme, without recompiling to swap. Three independent traits — LogBackend, OffsetStore, GroupCoordinatorBackend — each configured separately (default all memory://). Every backend exposes a BackendCapabilities descriptor so the broker can derive ApiVersions honestly and reject unsupported ops early.

Scope notes (from design review with codex):
- Log trait is structured (RecordBatchView + raw-bytes fast-path), NOT byte-opaque, because retention/compaction/timestamps/headers need parsed metadata.
- GroupCoordinatorBackend is richer than a simple load/save KV: it models live coordinator state (members, generations, assignments, sticky session semantics).
- Capability intersection is per-API/per-feature, not one global broker-wide meet. Startup validation for advertised ApiVersions; call-time rejection for per-request limits.
- Proxy-to-real-Kafka mode is explicitly out of scope — it needs a different trait family (UpstreamSession / CoordinatorBackend) and is a different architectural problem.

First durable backend lands as Postgres OffsetStore (smallest, highest value). Additional backends follow under separate beads.
notes:
Epic closed.

Evidence:
- All child beads closed (heimq-9e9f257c BackendCapabilities; heimq-04def9c7 OffsetStore trait; heimq-fb3e5ca8 GroupCoordinatorBackend trait; heimq-20daf864 LogBackend/TopicLog/PartitionLog; heimq-4ac4601c RecordBatchView; heimq-ed70c613 URL-scheme config + dispatch; heimq-c894fcda Postgres OffsetStore feature-gated; heimq-3af6652c kcat-stress integration matrix; heimq-24c6cd00 capability-derived ApiVersions; heimq-620861e8 reject unsupported ops at handler entry).
- Default config uses three memory backends; existing test suite passes (cargo test).
- Postgres OffsetStore ships behind the `postgres-offsets` feature flag with integration tests.
- Capability descriptor drives ApiVersions response via compute_supported_apis (src/protocol/mod.rs:105-120). Per-API intersection pinned by unit tests memory_default_advertises_full_supported_set, missing_group_coordinator_drops_only_group_apis, missing_offset_store_drops_only_offset_apis, compaction_false_does_not_leak_compaction_specific_apis (src/protocol/mod.rs tests module).
- API-001 contract (docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-kafka-protocol.md) refreshed to document capability-derived ApiVersions advertisement and reference compute_supported_apis.

description:
Today SUPPORTED_APIS in src/protocol/mod.rs is static. Now that each backend exposes capabilities, compute the effective advertised ApiVersions at startup by intersecting static protocol support with backend caps (e.g. don't advertise InitProducerId if no backend has transactions; don't advertise OffsetCommit if offsets backend isn't writable).

First durable backend. Stores committed offsets in a Postgres database. Feature-gated: `--features backend-postgres`.

  CREATE TABLE heimq_committed_offsets (
    group_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    topic TEXT NOT NULL,
    partition INT NOT NULL,
    committed_offset BIGINT NOT NULL,
    metadata TEXT,
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    PRIMARY KEY (group_id, topic, partition)
  );

- sqlx or tokio-postgres dependency, gated by feature.
- PostgresOffsetStore::connect(url) returns Arc<dyn OffsetStore>.
- BackendCapabilities: durability=WalFsync, survives_restart=true, transactions=false (this trait doesn't expose them), read_your_writes=true.
- URL scheme: postgres://user:pw@host:port/db?schema=heimq
- Schema initialize() idempotent at startup.

In-scope files:
- Cargo.toml (feature flag + sqlx dep)
- src/storage/postgres_offsets.rs (new)
- src/storage/dispatch.rs (register postgres://)
- tests/postgres_offsets.rs (integration test, uses a containerized postgres via testcontainers or skip if env var HEIMQ_PG_TEST_URL unset)
Depends on: OffsetStore trait, URL dispatch

acceptance: HARNESS-001-parity.md §Container Configuration references the actual TestServer API and process model, or test_support.rs adds the in-process entrypoint named in the design.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 0d3fb44.
File: heimq/docs/helix/02-design/HARNESS-001-parity.md:236-238
Category: correctness
Severity: high

Description:
Design says heimq is 'Started in-process via heimq::start_test_server() on an ephemeral port (reuses the test-support fixture already used in tests/integration.rs).' Two factual errors:
1. The actual API is heimq::test_support::TestServer::start() (and ::start_with_auto_create / ::start_with_partitions). No symbol heimq::start_test_server exists (verified via grep on heimq/src/test_support.rs:49-63).
2. TestServer is NOT in-process. heimq/src/test_support.rs:11,45,69-91 shows it spawns a subprocess via std::process::Command using CARGO_BIN_EXE_heimq and stores a Child handle. This affects lifecycle, port allocation, panic-cleanup, and observability assumptions for the harness.

Suggested fix:
Update §Container Configuration to reference heimq::test_support::TestServer::start() (or equivalent constructor) and describe it as a 'spawned subprocess fixture' rather than 'in-process'. If in-process semantics are actually required (e.g., for deterministic shutdown), call out building a new in-process entrypoint as work to be added to test_support.rs and adjust the Quality Gates accordingly.

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 8f2aeb8.
File: heimq/docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-kafka-protocol.md:27
Category: drift
Severity: medium

Description:
Commit 8f2aeb8 split the Request header bullet into 'non-flexible' and 'flexible v2' variants to align Transport and Framing with Version Policy on FEAT-006 flexible support. The parallel update was not made on the response side: line 27 still reads only '**Response header**: correlation_id.' with no mention of Response Header v1 (the flexible response header, which adds a TAG_BUFFER / tagged_fields block).

Per the Kafka wire protocol:
- Response Header v0: correlation_id
- Response Header v1: correlation_id, TAG_BUFFER (used by flexible-version responses; cf. KIP-482)

Flexible-version request/response headers come in pairs in Kafka and are encoded by the same kafka-protocol crate codec referenced in ADR-003. After the request-side fix, a reader of API-001 sees an asymmetric contract: requests get both header variants documented, responses do not. This is the same internal-contradiction class as the just-closed finding hx-708f92fe (Transport and Framing vs Version Policy), but in the inverse direction.

Note: ApiVersions is a documented Kafka quirk — it uses Response Header v0 even for flexible requests. Worth a footnote when the response header is split.

Suggested fix:
Update the Transport and Framing section to add a 'Response header (flexible v1)' bullet (correlation_id + tagged_fields block) in symmetry with the new flexible v2 request header bullet, referencing FEAT-006 and ADR-003. Optionally add a parenthetical noting that ApiVersions responses retain the v0 response header for compatibility.
title: drift: API-001 Response header missing flexible v1 variant after request-side update

acceptance: HARNESS-001-parity.md §Normalization Layer table or §Workload Driver Shape explicitly addresses CreateTime record timestamps; the diff harness produces zero diffs on the produce_fetch_roundtrip workload across two runs without manual exemption entries.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 0d3fb44.
File: heimq/docs/helix/02-design/HARNESS-001-parity.md:130-149
Category: correctness
Severity: medium

Description:
The normalization table covers log_append_time ('Replace with 0 when CreateTime semantics not in use') but not the record's own timestamp under CreateTime semantics. rdkafka producers default to setting message.timestamp.type=CreateTime via the producer's clock — the value is host wall-clock time at the moment of produce(). Two runs of produce_fetch_roundtrip (one against heimq, one against Redpanda) will record different CreateTime timestamps even though the workload is identical, producing spurious diffs on every observation.

The Observation enum carries the timestamp implicitly inside RecordConsumed if the harness records it; even if it does not, the harness still must decide whether to compare the value, normalize it, or ignore it.

Suggested fix:
Add a row to the normalization table: 'record CreateTime timestamp — Replace with sequential per-step index when CreateTime is in use; otherwise preserve.' Or, alternatively, document that record timestamps are explicitly excluded from the diff and update the RecordConsumed observation shape accordingly.
title: correctness: HARNESS-001 normalization table omits record-level CreateTime timestamps

acceptance: HARNESS-001-parity.md §Container Configuration specifies a non-floating image reference; the harness implementation passes that exact reference to testcontainers.
description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 0d3fb44.
File: heimq/docs/helix/02-design/HARNESS-001-parity.md:226-228
Category: correctness
Severity: medium

Description:
Design says: 'Image: docker.redpanda.com/redpandadata/redpanda:latest (pinned to a digest in tests/parity/redpanda-image.lock to prevent flake from upstream changes).' This is internally inconsistent:
- A ':latest' tag is by definition floating; it cannot be 'pinned'.
- testcontainers-rs takes an image string (name + tag/digest); it does not consult an external lockfile. A separate redpanda-image.lock cannot influence what testcontainers actually pulls.
- The NFR 'Less than 1% flake rate on the gating workload' depends on a stable image, so this gap matters.

Suggested fix:
Either (a) pin the image directly using a digest reference (e.g., docker.redpanda.com/redpandadata/redpanda@sha256:<digest>), with the lock file driving the digest used by the BrokerKind::Redpanda constructor; or (b) pin a specific version tag (e.g., :v25.x.y) with a renovate/dependabot job to bump it. Drop ':latest' from the design.
title: correctness: HARNESS-001 image pin strategy contradicts itself (':latest' tag with separate digest lock)

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 7ee598a (last-commit; addressing hx-37f63048).
File: heimq/docs/helix/02-design/HARNESS-001-parity.md:138 vs 75-76
Category: correctness / integration
Severity: medium

Description:
Commit 7ee598a adds a normalization rule for 'record CreateTime ts' (line 138):
  | record CreateTime ts | Replace with sequential per-step index when CreateTime semantics in use; otherwise preserve |

However, the §Workload Driver Shape RecordConsumed variant at lines 75-76 declares:
  RecordConsumed { key: Option<Bytes>, value: Option<Bytes>,
                   headers: Vec<(String, Bytes)>, partition: i32, offset: i64 }

There is no timestamp field on RecordConsumed. The normalization rule references a field the Observation enum does not capture, so the rule is unimplementable as written.

This is exactly the case the original finding hx-37f63048 anticipated: 'Or, alternatively, document that record timestamps are explicitly excluded from the diff and update the RecordConsumed observation shape accordingly.' The chosen path (table row) was applied without the corresponding shape update.

Suggested fix:
Add a 'timestamp: i64' (or 'timestamp: Option<i64>') field to the RecordConsumed variant in §Workload Driver Shape so the normalization rule has a target. Also, tighten the rule's placeholder format to match the convention used by producer_id ([pid:N]), e.g., '[ts:N]'.
title: correctness: HARNESS-001 normalization rule references field absent from Observation shape

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 911126b.
File: heimq/docs/helix/03-test/test-plan/test-plan.md
Lines: Phase 7-pre, first checkbox: 'Codec primitives: compact string, compact bytes, compact array, unsigned varint, signed (zigzag) varint, tagged-fields block — round-trip property tests.'
Category: drift
Severity: high

Description:
ADR-003 (accepted same epic, commit 3d79b92) explicitly decides that heimq will NOT implement codec primitives — they are delegated entirely to the kafka-protocol crate. CODEC-001 §'Codec Primitives' restates this: 'heimq does not implement compact-string, compact-array, unsigned-varint, signed-zigzag-varint, or tagged-fields codec primitives'.

The test-plan checkbox would either (a) test the upstream crate's primitive correctness (out of heimq's scope), or (b) require heimq to own primitive code that the ADR explicitly forbids. Either path wastes implementer effort and produces tautological or out-of-scope tests.

Suggested fix:
Replace the 'Codec primitives' bullet with checkboxes targeting heimq's actual codec surface per CODEC-001 §'Contract Validation':
- is_flexible(api_key, api_version) boundary table tests (off-by-one on each entry).
- decode_request: legacy header path remains green; new flexible header path parses client_id (legacy nullable string) and consumes tagged-fields trailer.
- encode_response: flexible header path emits empty tagged-fields trailer after correlation_id.
- encode_response: ApiVersions v3 special case — no tagged-fields trailer in response header.
- Router roundtrip at one flexible API version (correlation_id preserved; response decodes via kafka-protocol crate).

Add a one-line note: 'Codec primitive correctness (varints, compact strings, tagged fields) is delegated to the kafka-protocol crate per ADR-003.'
title: drift: test-plan Phase 7-pre demands codec primitive tests obsoleted by ADR-003

CODEC-001 specifies that for the flexible request header v2, client_id is read as a 'compact-nullable-string (unsigned varint for len+1, then len bytes; varint 0 = null)'. This contradicts both KIP-482 and FEAT-006 functional requirement #2, which state that request header v2 retains the legacy nullable-string format for client_id (i16-length-prefixed).

Verified against the upstream kafka-protocol crate v0.15.1 (the crate ADR-003 selected): src/messages/request_header.rs encodes client_id via types::String (legacy nullable string) at all supported versions, including v2:
    let client_id = if version >= 1 {
        types::String.decode(buf)?
    ...

If implemented per CODEC-001 as written, every flexible request from a modern client would mis-parse client_id and shift the tagged-fields block / body offset by an unpredictable amount, breaking all flexible APIs.

Suggested fix:
1. Update the 'Header Versions' table row for v2 to: 'api_key i16, api_version i16, correlation_id i32, client_id nullable-string (i16 len + bytes, same as legacy), tagged-fields block'.
2. Update §'Updated decode_request interface' item 4 to read: 'If flexible: reads client_id as i16-length-prefixed nullable string (same encoding as the legacy header), then reads and discards the tagged-fields block (varint count followed by tag/length/value tuples).'
3. Add an explicit cross-reference to FEAT-006 FR #2 ('client_id still as nullable string') and KIP-482 (rationale: header was already on-the-wire and could not be retroactively re-encoded).

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 1eba7bb (US-005 creation).
File: heimq/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-differential-parity-testing.md:51-52
Category: drift (feature spec vs child user story)
Severity: medium

Description:
The just-landed commit (hx-c9e1c85f) updated US-005 acceptance criteria to mark idempotent producers and transactions as gated on FEAT-002 acceptance, resolving the design-layer drift with HARNESS-001-parity.md. The same drift pattern still exists one level up: FEAT-003 functional requirement #5 reads 'Workloads cover all in-scope API surfaces: produce/fetch, consumer groups, idempotent producers, transactions.' with no note that two of the four are deferred until FEAT-002 lands.

FEAT-003 frontmatter does declare depends_on: FEAT-002, so the dependency is acknowledged at the feature level. But a fresh reader treating FEAT-003 FR#5 as the gating contract for HARNESS-001 will reach the same incorrect conclusion that hx-c9e1c85f flagged for the design layer. Authority order (PRD > Specs > Designs) requires FR#5 wording to match its child US-005, not the inverse.

Suggested fix:
Edit FEAT-003 FR#5 to mirror US-005's gating language. Example: 'Workloads cover produce/fetch and consumer groups as gating workloads at FEAT-003 acceptance; idempotent producers and transactions are added once FEAT-002 is accepted (see US-005 acceptance criteria and HARNESS-001-parity.md § Expansion path).'
title: drift: FEAT-003 FR#5 lists all 4 workloads as in-scope without noting idempotent/txn are gated on FEAT-002

description:
Review finding from fresh-eyes review of commit 0d3fb44.
File: heimq/docs/helix/02-design/HARNESS-001-parity.md:96-120, vs heimq/docs/helix/01-frame/user-stories/US-005-differential-parity-harness.md:18-21
Category: drift (design vs governing user story)
Severity: medium

Description:
US-005 acceptance criteria explicitly require 'The harness covers produce/fetch, consumer groups, idempotent producers, and transactions.' (US-005:21). The HARNESS-001 design implements only produce_fetch_roundtrip and consumer_group_lifecycle as initial workloads; idempotent_producer and transactions are listed under 'Expansion path (not in scope until FEAT-002 lands)' with a future status in exemptions.toml.

FEAT-003 frontmatter does declare 'depends_on: FEAT-002', so the dependency is pre-acknowledged at the feature level. But US-005 itself does not annotate the deferral, and a fresh reader treating US-005 as the gating spec for HARNESS-001 will conclude the design is incomplete. Authority order (Vision > PRD > Specs > Architecture > Designs) requires the design to either match the user story or to update the user story.

Suggested fix:
Either (a) update US-005 acceptance criteria to mark idempotent/transactional coverage as 'gated on FEAT-002 acceptance' (preferred — it stays accurate), or (b) split the deferred workloads into a sibling design (HARNESS-002) so HARNESS-001 itself can be marked 'satisfies US-005 gating workload only'.
title: drift: HARNESS-001 design scope diverges from US-005 acceptance criteria (idempotent/transactional workloads deferred)
